canonical_definition: The latent ability to identify and comprehend the relationships between different objects, such as interactions, spatial arrangements, or emotional connections.
papers: UniBench: Visual Reasoning Requires Rethinking Vision-Language Beyond Scaling

definitions.text: The latent ability to identify and comprehend the relationships between different objects, such as interactions, spatial arrangements, or emotional connections.
The ability to comprehend and represent relationships between objects within an image, such as spatial arrangements, actions, or attribute bindings.
